records.ety: [F. intemprance, L. intemperantia. See In- not, and Temperance.]

defs: The act of becoming, or state of being, intemperate; excess in any kind of action or indulgence; any immoderate indulgence of the appetites or passions.
quotes: God is in every creature; be cruel toward none, neither abuse any by intemperance.
Some, as thou sawest, by violent stroke shall die, By fire, flood, famine, by intemperance more In meats and drinks.

defs: Habitual or excessive indulgence in alcoholic liquors.
